tweets.full_text:
これをインストールすると、Claude Codeは完全に異なる感じになります。換言すると、Anthropicは静かに公式のプラグイン「claude-code-setup」という名前のものをリリースしました。このプラグインは、Claude Codeを実際のAI開発環境に変えます。プロジェクトをスキャンし、以下を推奨します：

→ フック
→ スキル
→ MCP サーバー
→ サブエージェント
→ 自動化

そして、全てを段階的に設定します。多くの人々はClaude Codeを完全にバニラで使用しています… そのため、彼らの経験は乱雑に感じられるのです。本当の力はその周りのエコシステムから来ます。インストールしてみてください：

/plugin install claude-code-setup@claude-plugins-official

お忘れにならないように、これをブックマークに登録しておきましょう。

user.name: ingrid souza
user.description: minha vida e de deus

tweets.full_text:
【いま私が使っているAI、解約したAI】

・GenSpark : 解約。半年くらい前までは一番使っていましたが、Claude Codeを使い始めてから使う機会がほぼなくなり解約。

・Cursor : 解約。GenSparkと同様。

・Claude Code : Proプラン（月額200ドル）→Proプラン（月額100ドル）にダウングレード。後述のCodexが良すぎて使う頻度が少し減り、月100ドルのプランで事足りるように。

・ChatGPT : Proプラン（月額16,800円〜）。GPT5.5になって急激に性能が高くなり、特にCodexでのブラウザ操作自動化や画像生成のクオリティは圧巻。システム開発はClaude Code、プラン作成や画像生成、業務自動化はCodexという感じで使い分け。動画編集もCodexでかなりのクオリティで自動化できるようになりました。

・CircleBack : 月額25ドル。最強の議事録ツール。月300本以上ミーティング議事録を録っていたら複数名での不正利用と判定されてしまったのか、先日一時的に利用制限になってしまい、サポートに連絡して全て自分のミーティングで使っていることを証明したら制限解除されました😅

・Canva : 解約。Adobe IllustratorとChatGPTの画像生成の組み合わせで事足りるので出番なしに。

他にも多数のAIツールを試してきていますが、今は上記の形に落ち着きました。AIの進化は驚異的なので、半年後にはまた構成が変わっている可能性はかなりありそう。

ちなみに添付の動画はCodexを使って、約2時間の動画からワンクリックで作成。「どの部分を切り抜くか？」の判断も自動。手作業での編集には劣りますが、これくらいのクオリティがポン出しできるなら十分かなと。凄い時代になりました…！

user.name: Nick Bonatsakis
user.description:
· Software entrepreneur, freelancer. iOS/Android/React Native.
· Building awesome apps and freelancing for awesome people.

quoted_status.full_text:
Anthropic has dove an unreal job at papering the earth with enterprise contracts; every company I walk into *just* went “all in on Claude” about to onboard hundreds or thousands of employees while every cutting edge builder I know has moved to codex. 

Speed of adoption compounds both ways, if you’re just catching up that cc and opus 4.6 are great, and have decided you’re decided, you’ll be even slower to see the frontier because you’re locked in to one provider’s view of the world. 

If you’re always one fragile step into the future, bop around models, know how codex and cowork and ai studio are all going to intersect, etc etc etc you’ll widen the gap of adoption, and adoption > impact.

To the fast & flexible go the spoils.

(But gg Claude!)

user.name: benmaritz
user.description: Affordable housing developer in Seattle, Tacoma and Portland. Dad, volunteer board member, aspiring pillar of the community

tweets.full_text:
Recent agentic systems (Claude Code, Codex, RLM, etc.) push context out of the prompt and into the environment (e.g., as files). This helps them maintain long-term knowledge about their goals and functionality.

🚨 While this is a good idea, we show a surprising result: systems that use external environments like this perform much better when given a small, fixed-size, in-context, agent-managed cache that "𝘱𝘦𝘦𝘬𝘴 𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘰" these environments.

🚀 Our paper, 𝗣𝗘𝗘𝗞: 𝙖 𝙨𝙮𝙨𝙩𝙚𝙢 𝙛𝙤𝙧 𝙗𝙪𝙞𝙡𝙙𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙢𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙩𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝗮𝗻 𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗰𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗲 𝙛𝙤𝙧 𝙇𝙇𝙈 𝙖𝙜𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙨, introduces this idea.

Compared with strong baselines, including RAG, Compaction Agents, and SOTA prompt-learning frameworks, PEEK dominates the cost–quality Pareto frontier: achieving +6.3–34.0% in quality, with fewer iterations and lower cost.

Paper: https://t.co/67pm4Dqbw5
GitHub: https://t.co/JNMehuzN9M

More in the thread below! (1/N)

SITUATION EXPLAINED: Anthropic bought Stainless because agents need rails.

Kong CEO @sonicaghi says the deal is about more than making Claude Code easier to use. Agents need SDKs, CLIs, MCP, and APIs to reach the systems where work actually happens.

"The connectivity infrastructure matters as much as the model... [it] is going to be the digital railroads on how AI moves."

"We're moving from pixels to APIs."

tweets.full_text:
6 hours. One video. CLAUDE.md, React + FastAPI, Skills, Hooks, MCP, sub-agents, parallel agent teams. From zero to agent orchestration in a single session.

Most Claude Code tutorials cover one feature in 20 minutes and pad the rest with intro slides. This one builds a full-stack app, connects it to GitHub and external tools, sets up background automations, and runs multiple agents at the same time.

The part worth skipping to if you already know the basics: MCP and sub-agents. That section alone covers what most paid courses never get to — how to make Claude talk to your actual infrastructure instead of just answering questions in a terminal.

Free. 6 hours. No paywall. The video is right here.

quoted_status.full_text:
Complete Claude Code Training: 13 HOURS.

I've searched everywhere. Nothing comparable exists.

Keep it bookmarked preciously 🔖

From A to Z: 

- CLAUDE.md: how to give Claude long-term memory for your project 
- Complete app: React frontend + Python FastAPI backend 
- Skills: reusable workflows triggered with a single command  
- Hooks: background automations  
- MCP: connect Claude to GitHub, browser, external tools 
- Sub-agents and specialized AI systems 
- Agent teams in parallel

All of it without writing a single line of code.( I transcribed it into French )

In the end: you use Claude Code like a pro and monetize your skills.

Beginner or advanced, everything is there in one place.

It's worth more than all those $500 courses you almost bought.

tweets.full_text:
1. One coding agent end-to-end (Claude Code or Codex). Use it daily, read its prompts, learn where it fails.
2. MCP basics. Build one filesystem + one git tool yourself before touching any framework.
3. One eval framework (promptfoo or Phoenix). No eval = no progress.

tweets.full_text:
6/ OpenClaude

WIIFM: one terminal workflow across model providers.

Pros: local/cloud providers, MCP, tools, agents, VS Code hooks.
Cons: fast-moving legal/compatibility edge, expect sharp corners.

Try if: you want Claude Code-style workflows without one-provider lock-in.
